items.meaningEn: absolutely necessary or extremely important
examples: Water is essential for life.
Good communication skills are essential in this role.
I think sleep is essential if you want to stay focused.

items.meaningEn: to delay doing something until a later time
examples: Don't put off your homework until Sunday.
We had to put off the meeting because of a client issue.
I keep putting off calling the bank, but I should do it today.

items.meaningEn: used to introduce a new subject or add information
examples: By the way, did you finish the report?
I'll send the file now. By the way, the deadline moved to Friday.
Nice to see you. By the way, I liked your presentation yesterday.

items.meaningEn: to choose something after thinking about it
examples: I need to make a decision by tonight.
Our manager made a decision to hire two more developers.
It's hard to make a decision when we don't have enough data.

items.meaningEn: to accept that you are accountable for something
examples: You should take responsibility for your mistakes.
As a team lead, she takes responsibility for the project's success.
I think everyone should take responsibility for their own learning.

items.meaningEn: to understand or solve something by thinking
examples: Can you help me figure out this math problem?
We need to figure out why the app is crashing in production.
I'm trying to figure out the best way to explain this to the client.

items.meaningEn: able to be trusted to do what is needed
examples: She is a reliable friend who always shows up on time.
We need a reliable supplier for our components.
In my opinion, being reliable is more important than being flashy.

items.meaningEn: to watch, listen to, or consider something carefully
examples: Please pay attention during the safety briefing.
Managers should pay attention to how their team members feel.
I wasn't paying attention, so I missed the key point.

items.meaningEn: over a long period of time in the future
examples: Exercise is good for you in the long run.
Cutting corners may save money now, but it hurts in the long run.
I believe learning English will help my career in the long run.

items.meaningEn: to think of an idea, plan, or solution
items.meaningVi: nghĩ ra, đưa ra ý tưởng
examples: We need to come up with a better plan.
She came up with a creative solution during the brainstorming session.
I couldn't come up with a good answer during the interview.

items.meaningEn: large or important enough to be noticed
examples: There was a significant improvement in her speaking skills.
The update caused a significant drop in page load time.
I think this is a significant step toward our goal.

items.meaningEn: to express worries about something
examples: Several employees raised concerns about the new policy.
I'd like to raise concerns about the project timeline.
Customers raised concerns about data privacy after the update.

items.meaningEn: in agreement about something
examples: Let's make sure we're on the same page before the meeting.
The design and engineering teams are finally on the same page.
I want to check that we're on the same page about the priorities.

items.meaningEn: to do or complete a task or plan
examples: The lab will carry out tests tomorrow.
Our team carried out a full review of the codebase.
We need to carry out the plan carefully to avoid mistakes.

items.meaningEn: evoking interest or attention in a powerful way
examples: She gave a compelling argument during the debate.
The case study presents a compelling reason to refactor the service.
I find his perspective compelling, even though I disagree with parts of it.

items.meaningEn: to make new information known
examples: The audit brought several issues to light.
Her research brought to light gaps in our onboarding process.
The interview brought to light concerns we hadn't considered.

items.expression: at the end of the day
items.meaningEn: when everything is considered
examples: At the end of the day, what matters is customer trust.
We can debate tools, but at the end of the day, delivery speed counts.
At the end of the day, I think we made the right call.

items.meaningEn: to decide something based on evidence or reasoning
examples: It's too early to draw a conclusion from one test.
After reviewing the metrics, we drew a conclusion about user retention.
I don't want to draw a conclusion without hearing both sides.

items.meaningEn: to make something less harmful, serious, or painful
examples: We added caching to mitigate performance issues.
The team proposed steps to mitigate security risks.
I think transparency can mitigate a lot of misunderstandings.

items.meaningEn: in a way that leaves no doubt
examples: The CEO stated unequivocally that quality comes first.
The data unequivocally supports the new approach.
I can say unequivocally that this was the right hire.

items.meaningEn: to be summarized as the main point
examples: The problem boils down to poor communication.
For me, the decision boils down to long-term impact.
Most debates about tooling boil down to team preference.

items.meaningEn: on the whole; generally speaking
examples: By and large, the rollout went smoothly.
By and large, employees supported the new remote policy.
I think, by and large, we've made solid progress this quarter.

items.meaningEn: to become more popular, accepted, or successful
items.meaningVi: được chấp nhận rộng rãi, có tiến triển
examples: The feature started to gain traction after the demo.
Our proposal is gaining traction with senior leadership.
I think this idea will gain traction once users see the benefits.

items.meaningEn: to feel pleased about something that is going to happen
examples: I look forward to the weekend.
We look forward to working with your team on this project.
I'm looking forward to hearing your thoughts on the proposal.

items.meaningEn: to remember something important when making a decision
examples: Keep in mind that the store closes at 9 p.m.
Keep in mind our budget when you suggest new features.
I want to keep in mind that not everyone learns at the same pace.
